reviews.review_description: This is a prototype for a 360 degree wave shooter. You can tell that a lot of thought has been put into the design. It certainly needs polish and more facets and features, but I love the start here and can't wait to see how it evolves.

An incredibly fun and immersive experience! Defending against incoming asteroids from all directions is thrilling, especially with the 360-degree shooting mechanics. The variety of weapons keeps gameplay fresh and exciting, and the accuracy is spot-on, making every shot satisfying. A perfect mix of action and strategy—highly recommended for anyone looking for an engaging VR shooter!

The game is well designed for comfortable game play. It's fun to play from one level to the next while earning new weapons. But it's also my first choice for a quick 5 or 10 minutes VR session. I highly recommend it.
Well done!

This is a fun comfortable 360 wave shooter  that will bring you back to your childhood playing asteroids only this time you are standing on a platform floating in space and the asteroids are coming from all directions around you.  For this game I set up the stationary circle boundary which pretty much matched up with the platform you are on although you can probably use either guardian as you don't move just really turn around in all directions shooting toward ceiling, floor and all around.  I found this very comfortable the way they have the platform shouldn't make people sick. You earn different guns by beating levels.  I thought this was beautiful environment, well polished game lots of fun and family friendly.
A great shooting game. I've unlocked fearless mode.
Yeah, old school - I really like it! It‘s real fun for the whole family 👍 Can be pretty addictive

Overall not very exciting. You are given a platform to stand on surrounded by a low quality skybox. Basic plasma guns to shoot the slow moving asteroids and random "UFOs". This would be way better if it was an actually VR version of Asteroids however it is not. There's a leaderboard but doesn't  really apply much incentive to grind up the boards. Replayability is low. I would not spend $9.99 on it.

Astroroxx feels like the classic game, Asteroid in VR, FPS style. It’s a casual experience with easy pick up and play controls. Great for parties and persons new to VR. It can be addictive fun, but also, with its limited gameplay offerings and repetition, I believe it can only be enjoyed by most in short bursts, as should be expected from casual wave shooters. Here’s the video review https://youtu.be/-5QVg4XhtY8
